---
title: "Estimate RNA velocity"
description: "Directional cell-state dynamics from spliced vs. unspliced counts."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 55
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

RNA velocity infers where each cell is heading by comparing **unspliced** (newly
transcribed, intron-containing) to **spliced** (mature) mRNA: a high unspliced
fraction means a gene is being upregulated. scVelo turns these ratios into a
velocity vector per cell, projected onto the UMAP.

## Options

| Component | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`velocity/velocyto`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/velocity/velocyto.qmd) | Extract spliced / unspliced counts from aligned BAMs (during ingestion) |
| [`velocity/scvelo`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/velocity/scvelo.qmd) | Compute velocity from an object that carries those layers |

## Example: scVelo

For example, once your object carries `spliced` / `unspliced` layers:

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/velocity/scvelo/main.nf \
  --input results/integrated.h5mu \
  --modality rna \
  --layer_spliced spliced \
  --layer_unspliced unspliced \
  --output velocity.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Key choices

- **Spliced / unspliced counts are a prerequisite.** They come from `velocyto`
  run against the aligned BAMs at [ingestion](ingest-10x.qmd) time, a standard
  count matrix doesn't carry them.
- `--n_top_genes`, `--n_principal_components`, and `--n_neighbors` tune the
  velocity graph, see the reference for defaults.

## Output

Velocity estimates and a velocity-projected embedding written into the object,
ready to plot as streamlines over the UMAP.
